item.title: Leaving the US.
item.description: We started this process 4 years ago through doing research and saving money. We hired an attorney 18 months back to help us with all paperwork and government forms. They also helped with arranging all the consulate appointments in person. Yes we could have done it without an attorney but frankly, we didn't want to. Now everything is finally coming together. Pet passports are in. Queen Mary II tickets are bought. Shipping containers will arrive in 2 months. House has a buyer. Visas just approved. Business loan closed on a new venture we are taking over. New home in under contract. We are doing this. 5 years from now we will be eligible to apply for citizenship. We didn't want to tell anyone in case things didn't work out. I didn't want to be like all those other people who simply talked about leaving. But now it's not all talk. We are doing it. We are leaving. And 3 days before my 30th, we will be in Scotland. Good luck to all my fellow Americans. I hope for the best back home but this is not a country I want to spend the rest of my life in anymore. And I need to get my girl out of it before we go full hand maids tale.
item.dc:creator: DirtyDusty

item.title: Comicon!!! (Cool people I saw)
item.description:
Don't forget to bring a towl
I found him!
The coolest kid there
Beefy!
...
Their chemistry was too good not to include this candid
Jarjar
She made her own wings
The delorian? I think?
This family
R2D2 wolf whistled me idk if the shot caught it tho
Blind dog seizure alerts for seeing eye human
Groovy
DAD BOD THOR
I legit SQUEEEed
Amazing self made costume
Wow my camera is AWFUL!
*wink*
Marty Poppins and Cruelliot DeVille
This kid was SO ANXIOUS. Hid behind his mom most of the con (saw him a few times) but he agreed to let me get his picture. He has ALL of his joints locked here. Brave little moon knight
(: this is the first pic I took
And finally, me. (Don't worry, I mask, just not for the pic) My tickets were nonrefundable, bought in January. I lasted 1 hour. But it was a fun hour. Gonna try again tomorrow.

item.title: The Top 7 Best News of Last Week
item.description:
1. Kitten rescued from inside Pepsi vending machine at Walmart store - The firefighters were able to find another opening in the machine and made visual contact with the feline. The rescuers were able to coax the cat to safety. The post said the kitten was adopted by the employee who originally heard the cat's cries.
2. Boy missing for eight days in Germany found alive in sewer - A passer-by heard a soft whimper coming from the direction of a manhole cover in the early hours and called emergency services. Rescuers rushed to the scene around 200m from his home and found Joe at the bottom of the sewer. A firefighter entered the sewer to bring him out. The child was taken to hospital suffering from hypothermia, but had no major injuries.
3. More Than 200,000 Borrowers Now Qualify for Student Debt Forgiveness - The Department of Education agreed to a $6 billion settlement to cancel debt for 200,000 borrowers. Borrowers will receive a full discharge of their loans, a refund, and credit repair. An additional 60,000 borrowers will have their cancellation decisions reviewed individually. 
4. Pride in London: More than a million attend 'biggest ever parade' - More than a million people have taken part in the 50th anniversary of the UK's first Pride parade in London. The parade paid homage to the original 1972 march, organised by the Gay Liberation Front (GLF), and saw revellers pass significant sites from the UK's LGBTQ+ movement.
5. Principal with his students turns Indian school into a green oasis with over 300 plant species - A government primary school in Firozabad’s Keethot village has become a centre of attraction for its lush greenery comprising over 300 species of plants, including a four-foot tall sandalwood tree. Spread over, 5,500 square feet area of school campus, the garden is a result of the efforts of the school headmaster Mohammad Shahid , who with the help of the students, has sown and nurtured plants that bear fruits and flowers, and vegetables used for preparing midday meals for the children.
6. Grand Canyon won't seek volunteers to kill bison this fall - A bison herd that lives almost exclusively in the northern reaches of Grand Canyon National Park won’t be targeted for lethal removal there this fall. Introducing the sound of gunfire and having people close to the bison was meant to nudge the massive animals back to the adjacent forest where they legally could be hunted. But the efforts had little effect. The park is now working with other agencies and groups on a long-term plan for managing the bison, an animal declared America’s national mammal in 2016 and depicted on the National Park Service logo.
7. Atlanta lawyers will rep anyone prosecuted for abortions for free - “If a doctor believes that it is appropriate to violate the law, at least as written, I want to be there to defend them because they need somebody in their corner and that is what I do,” he said Friday, hours after the Supreme Court released its opinion.
That's it for this week :) Subscribe to my newsletter where I post weekly good news from around the world https://feelgoodnews.substack.com/
item.dc:creator: FeelGoodNews

item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

item.title: We should ban poison
item.description: So...full story. She was forced by her parents to marry someone she didn't want to marry. Her boyfriend helped her poison a drink for her husband. Her husband didn't drink it. She put the poisoned drink in the fridge. Her mother-in-law used the poisoned drink to make a dessert, and served it at a party. Since she and her boyfriend made the poison, they were arrested. She was tried and given 15 life sentences concurrently, and has to pay a 3 million rupee fine. Provided she survives prison, she'll be released in 2033 2043.

item.title: Amazing isn't quite the word!
item.description: 17 year-old Mondo Duplantis has broken the world record outdoor pole vault in Stockholm with 6.16m. The slow motion of his record attempt is extraordinary.
item.dc:creator: treissuncia

item.title: POV: You are a frog, you wake up in your little mushroom house. You don't know what abortion is and you've never heard of politics.
item.description: You see a fly pass by, you take a big stretch and hop out after it as you start your day. Life is good and you've known nothing other than that. You are happy.

item.description: Your move Supreme Court. item.title: The ( 14 meter/ 45.9318 foot ), long Giant Squid is one of only two observed by humans in seas over the past decade, in Toyama Bay, Japan in 2015
item.description: It was first discovered in 2004 by Japanese scientists, often living at depths of up to 1,000 meters/ 3280.84 foot.

item.title: Not an average owner
item.description: I know I'm in the minority for business owners but we are out there. My employees are salaried with benefits at 37 hours a week. Everyone hates on bosses and managers on here but I want you all to know that you can still have a good manager/ boss that looks out for you.
